steps.prompt: Which is the main finite verb?

hints.datur: 'Datur' is a finite verb but belongs to the subordinate relative clause

hints.to eat: 'To eat' has the infinitive 'esse' (from 'edo'), identical to 'sum' — context determines 'to be'

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edo' (to eat) — same infinitive but different verb
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.exsto, exstare, exstiti, —: These are the parts of 'exsto' (to stand out/exist)

steps.reveal: datur = is given (3rd sing. pres. pass.)

hints.perfect passive indicative: Perfect passive would be 'datum est' — 'datur' is present passive
hints.present passive subjunctive: Present passive subjunctive would be 'detur' — 'datur' has -a- (indicative)
hints.future passive indicative: Future passive would be 'dabitur' — 'datur' is present

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'duco' (to lead)
hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the parts of 'dico' (to say)
hints.disco, discere, didici, —: These are the parts of 'disco' (to learn)

hints.accusative neuter: 'Hoc' looks the same in accusative neuter, but here it is the subject of 'est'
hints.ablative neuter: Ablative neuter would also be 'hoc', but context shows it is the subject
hints.dative: Dative of 'hic' would be 'huic' — 'Hoc' is nominative here

hints.accusative: 'Corpus' looks the same in accusative, but it is a predicate nominative after 'est'
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'corporis' — 'corpus' is nominative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'corporis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'corporis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'corporis' ends in -is

steps.reveal: pro vobis = for you / on your behalf (abl.)

hints.dative: 'Vobis' can be dative, but after 'pro' it must be ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative of 'vos' is 'vos' — 'vobis' after 'pro' is ablative
hints.genitive: Genitive of 'vos' is 'vestrum/vestri' — 'vobis' is dative or ablative

steps.reveal: quod pro vobis datur = which is given for you

hints.causal conjunction (because): 'Quod' can mean 'because', but here it refers back to 'corpus' as a relative pronoun
hints.conjunction introducing indirect speech: 'Quod' can introduce indirect speech in later Latin, but here it is a relative pronoun
hints.interrogative pronoun: 'Quod' as interrogative means 'which?' — here it is a relative pronoun

translation: This is my body which is given for you
